title: Review: diversified address + WalletFvks payment wiring
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — The only runtime-visible defect is the PAYMENT_ACCOUNT doc comment placement (nie-0o9.1), which is a documentation error with no operational impact. The advance_diversifier_to doc promise for idx=0 (nie-0o9.2) is a latent trap but unreachable given current callers. Everything else is style/consistency. The core logic (monotonic advance, fatal-on-skip, generate_fresh_address abstraction) is correct and well-tested.
Confidence: 8% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) cannot verify the sapling-crypto find_address bound behavior under adversarially constructed FVKs — the 256-attempt limit is a ZIP-32 convention but the actual distribution of invalid diversifiers for real-world seeds is untested; (2) advance_diversifier_to correctness under concurrent access is asserted-by-comment not by test — if max_connections is ever changed the code breaks silently. Would raise confidence if: a property test randomizing FVK seeds and verifying distinctness across 10000 consecutive calls were added; and if advance_diversifier_to were converted to use a transaction (matching next_diversifier).
